zh.Grove in South Brent, Axbridge, Burnham,: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特達爾
zh.Blackford, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特布拉可福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Longland於大約1353年出生在英國薩默塞特達爾時，她的父親John de Longland32歲，母親Isabel Beaupré15歲。她於1392年與John Roynon在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1421年在英國薩默塞特享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Longland于大约1353年出生在英国薩默塞特達爾时，她的父亲John de Longland32岁，母亲Isabel Beaupré15岁。她于1392年与John Roynon在英国薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1421年在英国薩默塞特享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Longland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from any of the many minor places in England called Longland(s) or Langland(s). There has probably been some confusion with the Scottish surname   Langlands  .

variants.name: Langland
meanings: English: topographic name from Old English lang, long ‘long’ + land ‘land’, ‘territory’.
Scottish: habitational name from Langlands in Guthrie (Angus).

variants.name: Langlands
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Langlands in Guthrie (Angus).

variants.name: Longlands
meanings: English, Scottish: variant of Longland with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. There has probably been some confusion with the Scottish surname Langlands .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Longland was born about 1353, in Cheddar, Somerset, England, her father, John de Longland, was 32 and her mother, Isabel Beaupré, was 15. She married John Roynon in 1392, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1421, in Somerset, England, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Longland est née environ 1353, à Cheddar, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John de Longland, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Isabel Beaupré, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé John Roynon en 1392, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1421, à Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Longland um 1353 in Cheddar, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Longland 32 und ihre Mutter Isabel Beaupré 15. Sie hat 1392 in Somerset, England John Roynon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1421 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Longland è nata il circa 1353 nel Cheddar, Somerset, England, sua padre, John de Longland, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Isabel Beaupré, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato John Roynon nel 1392, nel Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1421, nel Somerset, England, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Longlandが約1353年にイギリスソメルセトチェッドアルで生まれた時，父John de Longlandは32歳，母Isabel Beaupréは15歳であった。1392年にイギリスソメルセトでJohn Roynonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1421年にイギリスソメルセトで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Longland(이)가 대략 1353년 영국 서머세트 체더르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Longland(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Beaupré(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1392년 영국 서머세트에서 John Roynon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1421년 영국 서머세트에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Longland nasceu aproximadamente 1353, em Cheddar, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Longland, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Beaupré, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com John Roynon em 1392, em Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1421, em Somerset, Inglaterra, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Longland родилась около 1353 г. в местности чеддар, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Longland было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Beaupré, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Roynon в 1392 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1421 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Longland nació aproximadamente 1353, en Cheddar, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Longland, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Isabel Beaupré, tenía 15 años. Se casó con John Roynon en 1392, en Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1421, en Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 69 años.
